# Markdown Quality Checklist

Use this checklist before finalizing documentation changes.

## Content
- Purpose is clear in the first section
- Key decisions and constraints are captured
- Facts are supported by source material
- Open questions are explicitly listed

## Structure
- Single H1 title
- Logical H2 and H3 hierarchy
- No skipped heading levels
- Long sections split into smaller chunks

## Language
- Audience-appropriate tone
- Concise sentences and minimal filler
- Terms are used consistently
- No ambiguous pronouns for critical actions

## Actionability
- Next steps are concrete and owner-friendly
- TODOs are specific and testable
- Summary includes what changed and why

## Formatting
- Lists render cleanly
- Code blocks have language tags when relevant
- Relative links resolve correctly
- File names and commands are formatted consistently
